text: I was tired of my own questions and the fear that wouldn't fade. I carried all my worry till my shoulders nearly broke, and I whispered to the silence with the little faith I spoke. It was smaller than a mustard seed, it was barely even there, but it turned me toward the only light still burning anywhere. I was empty, I was lonely, with the dark on every side, when a voice said, open up the word, and I had nowhere left to hide. So I read it in the silence, and the words began to shine, and the fear I couldn't carry was trading line by line. Now there's peace I cannot explain where the worry used to be, he took the weight I couldn't hold and he carried it for me. So I read it in the morning, and I read it in the night, and the words I almost gave up on began to set me right. Every promise like a hand held out to pull me from the deep, come to me you weary one, and he gave me rest to keep. I was empty, I was lonely, with the dark on every side, when a voice said, open up the word, and I had nowhere left to hide. So I read it in the silence, and the words began to shine, and the fear I couldn't carry was trading line by line. Now there's peace I cannot explain where the worry used to be, he took the weight I couldn't hold and he carried it for me. I don't have a faith of mountains, I just have a faith of seas, but it found me in the wreckage and it gave me what I need. And I'm grateful for the morning, I'm grateful for the gift, for the hand that took my fear away and gave me peace to live. I was empty, I was lonely, with the dark on every side, when a voice said, open up the word, and I had nowhere left to hide. So I read it in the silence, and the words began to shine, and the fear I couldn't carry was trading line by line. Now there's peace I cannot explain where the worry used to be, he took the weight I couldn't hold and he carried it for me. So I'll drive into the daylight with the windows rolling down, and the word that came to find me is the peace I finally found.

I carried all my worry till my shoulders nearly broke, and I whispered to the silence with the little faith I spoke.
